description_long: This is a small, realistic crab with a robust, rounded carapace and two prominent claws, one of which is noticeably larger than the other. The carapace is dark brown with mottled patterns, while the underside and joints of the legs are lighter, almost white or pale pink. The crab has eight visible walking legs, each jointed and covered in a slightly rough texture, and two large chelae (claws) that are a lighter color with reddish tinges. Its eyes are set on short stalks at the front of the carapace, giving it a wide field of view. The overall surface appears hard and slightly glossy, typical of a crustacean exoskeleton. This crab would be found in urban environments near water features, parks, or seafood markets.
description: A small, dark brown crab with a rounded carapace, two large claws (one much larger), and eight jointed legs. The surface is hard, slightly glossy, and mottled with lighter tones on the underside.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's face, both claws raised, and the full width of the carapace.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the size difference between the two claws and the segmentation of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the claws and the curvature of the body, with more detail on the leg joints.
